full_text_rationale: Full text evaluates Memory Transfer Learning for coding agents, using unified memory pools from heterogeneous coding domains and measuring cross-domain transfer across six coding benchmarks.
extractor_notes: Fresh search architecture extraction batch 011; controlled-comparison and quality status remain pending aggregate recomputation.
autores: Kangsan Kim, Minki Kang, Taeil Kim, Yanlai Yang, Mengye Ren, Sung Ju Hwang
titulo: Memory Transfer Learning: How Memories are Transferred Across Domains in Coding Agents
formato_armazenamento: Unified heterogeneous-domain memory pools storing four representations: Trajectory command/action histories, Workflow action sequences, Summary task and experience summaries, and Insight task-agnostic memory items with title, description, and content.
framework_agente: Memory Transfer Learning experimental coding-agent framework using mini-swe-agent, offline memory generation, cross-domain memory pools, and embedding retrieval
metodo_avaliacao: benchmark; cross-domain transfer study; self-evolving baseline comparison; negative-transfer analysis; memory-abstraction analysis; memory-pool scaling; cross-model transfer; retrieval-method ablation
benchmarks_utilizados: LiveCodeBenchv6, Aider-Polyglot, SWE-Bench Verified, TerminalBench2, ReplicationBench, MLGym-Bench
metricas_desempenho: Pass@3, average score, Pass@1 in appendix/scaling/cross-model studies, memory count, memory-pool size, number of source domains
metricas_cl: Memory type comparison across Trajectory, Workflow, Summary, and Insight; cross-domain transfer; cross-model transfer; negative-transfer categories; abstraction and retrieval analysis
baseline_comparado: Zero-shot coding agent, ReasoningBank, AgentKB, memory retrieval variants with LLM reranking and adaptive rewriting
tamanho_memoria_ao_longo_tempo: In the self-evolving comparison, MTL uses 431 memories versus ReasoningBank's 97 and AgentKB's 5,899. Inference retrieves three memories per query from the heterogeneous-domain pool.
resultado_principal: Cross-domain memory improves average performance by 3.7% for GPT-5-mini, with the strongest gains from high-level Insight memories rather than low-level trajectory memories.
desempenho_vs_baseline: With GPT-5-mini, zero-shot average Pass@3 is 0.523 and MTL with Insight reaches 0.560. MTL outperforms ReasoningBank and AgentKB on the three-benchmark comparison with average 0.630 versus 0.601 and 0.613.
custo_vs_baseline: MTL uses fewer stored memories than AgentKB in the self-evolving comparison (431 versus 5,899) while reaching a higher average score; no monetary or token-cost comparison is reported.
limitacoes: Memory transfer can cause negative transfer through domain-mismatched anchors, false validation confidence, and misapplied best-practice transfer; advanced static retrieval strategies underperform simple embedding similarity.
ameacas_validade: Results depend on sampled benchmark tasks, GPT-5-mini memory generation and judging, embedding retrieval, source-domain composition, memory format prompts, cross-model transfer setup, and whether benchmark domains represent real deployment diversity.
